# Post-mortem: <incident title> (YYYY-MM-DD)

- **Status:** Draft <!-- Draft | Final -->
- **Authors:** <names>
- **Severity:** <SEV1 critical | SEV2 major | SEV3 minor>

> **Blameless.** The goal is *learning*, not blame. We assume everyone acted in
> good faith with the information they had. Focus on systems and processes, not
> individuals.

## Summary

2–3 sentences: what happened, who/what was impacted, and for how long.

## Impact

- **Users affected:** how many / which.
- **Duration:** detection → resolution.
- **What broke:** concrete user-visible effect.

## Timeline (UTC)

| Time | Event |
|---|---|
| YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM | … |

## Root cause

The underlying cause — not just the trigger. Use "5 whys" if useful: keep asking
*why* until you reach a systemic cause, not a person.

## Detection

How did we find out? Could we have found out sooner?

## Resolution

What actually fixed it, and how we verified recovery.

## Lessons learned

- **What went well.**
- **What went badly.**
- **Where we got lucky.**

## Action items

| Action | Type (prevent / detect / mitigate) | Owner | Tracking |
|---|---|---|---|
| … | … | … | <issue link> |
